Generated Narrative: ValueSet ACCESSCKMDiagnosisVS

This value set includes codes based on the following rules:

  • Include codes fromhttp://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm version Not Stated (use latest from terminology server) where concept is-a E08 (Diabetes mellitus due to underlying condition)
  • Include codes fromhttp://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm version Not Stated (use latest from terminology server) where concept is-a E09 (Drug or chemical induced diabetes mellitus)
  • Include codes fromhttp://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm version Not Stated (use latest from terminology server) where concept is-a E11 (Type 2 diabetes mellitus)
  • Include codes fromhttp://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm version Not Stated (use latest from terminology server) where concept is-a E13 (Other specified diabetes mellitus)
  • Include these codes as defined in http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm version Not Stated (use latest from terminology server)

    N18.30Chronic kidney disease, stage 3 unspecified

    N18.32Chronic kidney disease, stage 3b
  • Include codes fromhttp://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm version Not Stated (use latest from terminology server) where concept is-a I25.7 (Athscl CABG and cor art of transplanted heart w ang pctrs)
  • Include codes fromhttp://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm version Not Stated (use latest from terminology server) where concept is-a G45 (Transient cerebral ischemic attacks and related syndromes)

This value set excludes codes based on the following rules:

  • Exclude these codes as defined in http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm version Not Stated (use latest from terminology server)

name: ACCESSCKMDiagnosisVS
title: ACCESS Cardio-Kidney-Metabolic (CKM) Track Qualifying Diagnoses
description: This value set contains ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes that qualify a patient for the ACCESS Model Cardio-Kidney-Metabolic (CKM) track. Includes diabetes mellitus (all types), atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease (ASCVD), and chronic kidney disease (CKD stage 3) diagnoses.
copyright: This value set includes content from ICD-10-CM, which is copyright World Health Organization (WHO). ICD-10-CM codes are freely available in the public domain.
